parameters.content:
### When Chat Message Received

Opens a hosted chat session; every user message starts an execution with `chatInput` + `sessionId`.

Next: Normalize Request Payload

### Normalize Request Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming request records to a stable schema

Next: Reason & Act (AI Agent)

### Reason & Act (AI Agent)

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Shape Agent Output Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

âš  failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `languageModel`

ðŸ”‘ Groq API key

### Search Reference Wiki

Reference lookup tool for general-knowledge questions.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Custom Logic Tool

Deterministic code helper the agent can invoke for exact computation.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Lookup API Data

Tool the agent can call to hit the REST API with parameters it chooses at runtime.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Think It Through

Scratchpad tool: lets the agent reason step-by-step before acting.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `tool`

### Conversation Memory

Conversation memory keyed by session â€” the bot remembers earlier turns for each user separately.

Feeds **Reason & Act (AI Agent)** as `memory`

### Shape Agent Output

Shapes the item: writes `answer`, `confidence`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Confident Enough?

### Confident Enough?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.confidence }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Deliver Answer + Log to Google Sheets Â· â‘¡â†’ Escalate to Human Queue

### Deliver Answer + Log to Google Sheets

Appends a new row to 'Request Tracker' â€” a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Join Outcomes Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

â†» retries 3Ã—/2s Â· âš  failures exit via the error rail Â· ðŸ”‘ Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Deliver Answer + Log to Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Escalate to Human Queue

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) â€” a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Join Outcomes Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

â†» retries 3Ã—/2s Â· âš  failures exit via the error rail Â· ðŸ”‘ Slack OAuth2

### Join Outcomes

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per request (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats â€” the idempotency guard.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Request Tracker' â€” a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: â‘ â†’ Send Summary to Slack Â· â‘¡â†’ Capture Failure Context

â†» retries 3Ã—/2s Â· âš  failures exit via the error rail Â· ðŸ”‘ Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) â€” a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

â†» retries 3Ã—/2s Â· âš  failures exit via the error rail Â· ðŸ”‘ Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) â€” a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

# Answer chat messages 24/7

**Pattern:** Chatbot Responder Â· **Level:** Production Â· **Trigger:** Chat

## What it does
Answer chat messages 24/7. It responds live to chat messages.

## Why it exists
Manual research assistant is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
When Chat Message Received â†’ Normalize Request Payload â†’ Reason & Act (AI Agent) â†’ Shape Agent Output â†’ Confident Enough? â†’ Deliver Answer + Log to Google Sheets â†’ Join Outcomes â†’ â€¦

## Use it for
- Teams that want to chatbot responder without manual effort
- Anyone automating research assistant in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Responds in seconds
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Groq API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: ðŸŸ¦ entry Â· ðŸŸª AI Â· ðŸŸ« actions Â· â¬œ utilities Â· ðŸŸ© outputs Â· ðŸŸ¥ error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath â€” read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger â€” hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click â†’ Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Uses an AI Agent, not a fixed Chain, because the assistant must choose which tool answers the question â€” a Chain would need one hard-coded branch per tool. The 'Confident Enough?' gate after the agent routes low-confidence answers to a human queue instead of shipping an uncertain answer: a little latency traded for not eroding trust.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade â€” deepen your understanding:

1. Add one more tool to the pool with a clear one-line description and watch how the agent decides to use it.
2. Change the confidence threshold on 'Confident Enough?' and see how often answers get escalated to a human.
3. Swap the Think tool for a Vector Store tool wired to a real knowledge base and turn this into RAG.

## ðŸ›Ÿ Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3Ã— with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail â†’ context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ðŸ¤– AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens â€” keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## ðŸ§¹ Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node â†’ 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## ðŸ›  Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see ðŸ”‘ lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your research assistant process. Every node card explains its role â€” safe to extend at any marked branch.
